name: Sir Alf Ramsey
summary: The architect of England's only major trophy. Ramsey reshaped the team's tactics around a flexible 4-3-3 without wingers, dubbed the 'Wingless Wonders', and delivered the 1966 World Cup on home soil. He remains the only England manager to win a major tournament.

name: Sir Bobby Robson
summary: Robson took England to the World Cup quarter-finals in 1986 and the semi-finals in 1990, both agonisingly close to glory. The 1990 run, built on a young, attacking side, is remembered as one of English football's great near-misses.

name: Terry Venables
summary: Venables led England to the Euro 1996 semi-finals on home soil, playing some of the most fluent football of the era, before penalties ended the run against Germany at Wembley.

name: Gareth Southgate
summary: England's longest-serving manager of the modern era. Southgate — whose own playing career included the missed penalty that ended Euro 1996 — rebuilt the national team's culture and reached a World Cup semi-final and two European Championship finals, more major-tournament football than any England manager since Ramsey. He resigned after the Euro 2024 final defeat to Spain.

name: Thomas Tuchel
summary: The first overseas manager to take permanent charge of the England men's team. A Champions League winner with Chelsea in 2021, Tuchel won all eight of his World Cup qualifiers before leading England into their first World Cup semi-final since 2018 at the 2026 tournament.
